examples.title: Simple Synchronous Request-Reply Client
examples.description: Basic UDP client that sends request and waits for response with timeout
examples.code:
FUNCTION sendRequest(serverAddress, message):
 socket = createUDPSocket()
 transactionID = generateUniqueID()
 request = createMessage(transactionID, message)
 
 SET timeout = 5 seconds
 retryCount = 3
 
 WHILE retryCount > 0:
 send(socket, request, serverAddress)
 startTimer()
 
 WHILE timer < timeout:
 IF available(socket):
 response = receive(socket)
 IF response.transactionID == transactionID:
 close(socket)
 RETURN response.data
 
 checkTimer()
 
 retryCount = retryCount - 1
 log("Timeout, retrying...")
 
 close(socket)
 RETURN error("Request timed out")
examples.explanation: Implements synchronous request-reply over UDP with timeout and retry logic. Uses transaction ID to match responses to requests.

examples.title: Asynchronous Request with Callback
examples.description: Shows how to handle request-reply without blocking using callbacks/promises
examples.code:
FUNCTION sendAsyncRequest(serverAddress, message, callback):
 socket = createUDPSocket()
 transactionID = generateUniqueID()
 
 pendingRequests[transactionID] = callback
 request = createMessage(transactionID, message)
 
 send(socket, request, serverAddress)
 scheduleCleanup(transactionID, timeout=10s)

FUNCTION onResponseReceived(response):
 transactionID = response.transactionID
 callback = pendingRequests[transactionID]
 
 IF callback != null:
 callback(response.data)
 remove(pendingRequests, transactionID)
 ELSE:
 log("Unknown transaction, possibly stale")

FUNCTION cleanupTask(transactionID):
 IF transactionID in pendingRequests:
 callback = pendingRequests[transactionID]
 callback(error("Request timed out"))
 remove(pendingRequests, transactionID)
examples.explanation: Demonstrates asynchronous handling where client registers callback and continues execution. Response processing happens via callback when response arrives.

examples.title: RPC Server Skeleton
examples.description: Server-side stub that unmarshals requests, dispatches to implementation, and marshals response
examples.code:
CLASS RPCServerSkeleton:
 FUNCTION registerService(serviceName, implementation):
 services[serviceName] = implementation
 
 FUNCTION handleRequest(rawRequest):
 header = unmarshalHeader(rawRequest)
 body = unmarshalBody(rawRequest)
 
 serviceName = header.serviceName
 methodName = header.methodName
 transactionID = header.transactionID
 
 service = services.get(serviceName)
 IF service == null:
 RETURN createErrorResponse(transactionID, "Service not found")
 
 // Check for duplicate request (idempotency)
 IF isDuplicate(transactionID):
 cachedResponse = getCachedResponse(transactionID)
 IF cachedResponse != null:
 RETURN cachedResponse
 
 // Invoke the actual method
 result = invoke(service, methodName, body.parameters)
 
 response = marshalResponse(transactionID, result)
 
 // Cache for idempotency
 cacheResponse(transactionID, response)
 
 RETURN response

FUNCTION invoke(service, methodName, params):
 method = getMethod(service, methodName)
 RETURN method.invoke(params)
examples.explanation: Server skeleton handles request parsing, duplicate detection for idempotency, method dispatch, and response marshalling.
